perturbation.original_text: II. Non-disclosure Obligations . The Receiving Party promises and agrees to receive and hold the Confidential Information in confidence. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Receiving Party further promises and agrees: A. to protect and safeguard the Confidential Information against unauthorized use, publication or disclosure; B. not to use any of the Confidential Information except for the Business Purposes. C. not to, directly or indirectly, in any way, reveal, report, publish, disclose, transfer or otherwise use any of the Confidential Information except as specifically authorized by the Disclosing Party in accordance with this Non-Disclosure Agreement. D. not to use any Confidential Information to unfairly compete or obtain unfair advantage vis-a-vis Disclosing Party in any commercial activity which may be comparable to the commercial activity contemplated by the parties in connection with the Business Purposes. E. to restrict access to the Confidential Information to those of its officers, directors, and employees who clearly need such access to carry out the Business Purposes. F. to advise each of the persons to whom it provides access to any of the Confidential Information, that such persons are strictly prohibited from making any use, publishing or otherwise disclosing to others, or permitting others to use for their benefit or to the detriment of the Disclosing Party, any of the Confidential Information, and, upon Request of the Disclosing Party, to provide the Disclosing Party with a copy of a written agreement to that effect signed by such persons. G. to comply with any other reasonable security measures requested in writing by the Disclosing Party. H. To refrain from directly contacting or communicating by whatsoever means to the Source(s) of Information without written consent of the Disclosing Party. I. To undertake not to disclose any names and their particulars to third parties without the written consent by the Disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: II. Non-disclosure Obligations . The Receiving Party promises and agrees to receive and hold the Confidential Information in confidence, unless obligated by law or regulatory authority. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Receiving Party further promises and agrees: A. to protect and safeguard the Confidential Information against unauthorized use, publication or disclosure, unless compelled by law; B. not to use any of the Confidential Information except for the Business Purposes, unless required by a legal mandate. C. not to, directly or indirectly, in any way, reveal, report, publish, disclose, transfer or otherwise use any of the Confidential Information except as specifically authorized by the Disclosing Party in accordance with this Non-Disclosure Agreement or as required by law. D. not to use any Confidential Information to unfairly compete or obtain unfair advantage vis-a-vis Disclosing Party in any commercial activity which may be comparable to the commercial activity contemplated by the parties in connection with the Business Purposes, except when disclosure is legally required. E. to restrict access to the Confidential Information to those of its officers, directors, and employees who clearly need such access to carry out the Business Purposes, unless a broader disclosure is mandated by legal authorities. F. to advise each of the persons to whom it provides access to any of the Confidential Information, that such persons are strictly prohibited from making any use, publishing or otherwise disclosing to others, or permitting others to use for their benefit or to the detriment of the Disclosing Party, any of the Confidential Information, and, upon Request of the Disclosing Party, to provide the Disclosing Party with a copy of a written agreement to that effect signed by such persons; however, these restrictions are superseded by legal obligations to disclose such information. G. to comply with any other reasonable security measures requested in writing by the Disclosing Party, provided that these measures do not conflict with legal disclosure requirements. H. To refrain from directly contacting or communicating by whatsoever means to the Source(s) of Information without written consent of the Disclosing Party, except when required by a court order. I. To undertake not to disclose any names and their particulars to third parties without the written consent by the Disclosing party, unless legally compelled to do so.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds numerous exceptions to the non-disclosure obligations, stating that disclosure is permitted if 'obligated by law or regulatory authority.' This directly contradicts the core intent of the NDA, which is to maintain confidentiality. Section XIV, Remedies, states that any disclosure is a breach. The new pertubation of II, creates an in-text contradiction, where disclosure is permitted due to legal requirements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: XIV. Remedies . The Receiving Party understands and acknowledges that any disclosure or misappropriation of any of the Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement may cause the Disclosing Party irreparable harm, the amount of which may be difficult to ascertain and, therefore, agrees that the Disclosing Party shall have the right to apply to a court of competent jurisdiction for an order restraining any such further disclosure or misappropriation and for such other relief as the Disclosing Party shall deem appropriate. Such right of the Disclosing Party shall be in addition to Remedies otherwise available to the Disclosing Party at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section II introduces exceptions to the non-disclosure obligations, allowing disclosure if 'obligated by law or regulatory authority.' This directly contradicts Section XIV, which states that 'any disclosure or misappropriation' is a violation. The addition of these exceptions creates a clear conflict between the obligation to maintain confidentiality and the allowance for legally mandated disclosures, causing an in-text contradiction as the agreement now both prohibits and permits disclosure.

perturbation.original_text: XIII. Term and Termination . This Agreement shall commence on the date first written above. The Receiving Party's right to use the Confidential Information in connection with the Business Purposes shall continue in effect until the period of one year from the date above or the other Party provides the Receiving Party with written notice of termination of such right, whichever is earlier. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party's obligations with respect to the Confidential Information hereunder shall continue in full force for at least five years from the termination date and/or effect until further notice from the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: XIII. Term and Termination . This Agreement shall commence on the date first written above. The Receiving Party's right to use the Confidential Information in connection with the Business Purposes shall continue in effect until the period of one year from the date above or the other Party provides the Receiving Party with written notice of termination of such right, whichever is earlier. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party's obligations with respect to the Confidential Information hereunder shall cease immediately upon termination of the Business Purposes, unless a separate agreement is reached.
perturbation.explanation: This modifies the term and termination clause. Originally, the confidentiality obligations extended for five years. Now, they cease immediately upon termination of the Business Purposes unless another agreement is present. This contradicts Section IV, Return of Confidential Information, which implies an ongoing obligation to return materials after termination, suggesting confidentiality should still be in effect. The original text in section XIII talks about what would happen after the agreement, where it's altered to indicate the end of the business purpose, and contradic Section IV, return of Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: IV. Return of Confidential Information . The Receiving Party agrees, upon termination of the Business Purposes or upon the written request of the other Party, whichever is earlier, to promptly deliver to the other Party all records, notes, and other written, printed, or tangible materials in the possession of the Receiving Party, embodying or pertaining to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section XIII alters the termination clause, stating that confidentiality obligations cease immediately upon termination of the Business Purposes unless a separate agreement is reached. This directly contradicts Section IV, which requires the Receiving Party to return all confidential information upon termination of the Business Purposes. The original Section XIII maintained confidentiality for five years, which aligned with the return obligation in Section IV. The change creates a strong inconsistency because the obligation to return confidential information implies that confidentiality is still in effect, while the modified Section XIII states that it ceases immediately.

perturbation.original_text: V. No Right to Confidential Information . A. The Receiving Party hereby agrees and acknowledges that no license, either express or implied, is hereby granted to the Receiving Party by the other Party to use any of the Confidential Information. B. The Receiving Party further agrees that all inventions, improvements, copyrightable works and designs relating to machines, methods, compositions, or products of the other Party directly resulting from or relating to the Confidential Information and the right to market, use, license and franchise the Confidential Information or the ideas, concepts, methods or practices embodied therein shall be the exclusive property of the other Party, and the Receiving Party has no right or title thereto.
perturbation.changed_text: V. No Right to Confidential Information . A. The Receiving Party hereby agrees and acknowledges that no license, either express or implied, is hereby granted to the Receiving Party by the other Party to use any of the Confidential Information, except as required for regulatory compliance. B. The Receiving Party further agrees that all inventions, improvements, copyrightable works and designs relating to machines, methods, compositions, or products of the other Party directly resulting from or relating to the Confidential Information and the right to market, use, license and franchise the Confidential Information or the ideas, concepts, methods or practices embodied therein shall be the exclusive property of the other Party, and the Receiving Party has no right or title thereto, unless explicitly agreed otherwise in writing for specific use cases.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces exceptions to the clause denying rights to confidential information. By adding 'except as required for regulatory compliance' and 'unless explicitly agreed otherwise in writing for specific use cases,' it contradicts the earlier statement that 'no license, either express or implied, is hereby granted'. The original text in V.A states that the receiving party has no express or implied rigths, and the new pertubation creates an in text contradition where there's an exception to comply with regulatory purposes
perturbation.contradicted_text: V. No Right to Confidential Information . A. The Receiving Party hereby agrees and acknowledges that no license, either express or implied, is hereby granted to the Receiving Party by the other Party to use any of the Confidential Information, except as required for regulatory compliance. B. The Receiving Party further agrees that all inventions, improvements, copyrightable works and designs relating to machines, methods, compositions, or products of the other Party directly resulting from or relating to the Confidential Information and the right to market, use, license and franchise the Confidential Information or the ideas, concepts, methods or practices embodied therein shall be the exclusive property of the other Party, and the Receiving Party has no right or title thereto, unless explicitly agreed otherwise in writing for specific use cases.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces exceptions ('except as required for regulatory compliance' and 'unless explicitly agreed otherwise in writing for specific use cases') to the original statement that 'no license, either express or implied, is hereby granted'. This directly contradicts the initial clause, creating a strong in-text contradiction as the contradicted text is exactly the same as the changed text, which modifies the original text. The addition of exceptions alters the core meaning of the original clause.

perturbation.original_text: X. Communication : The two parties agree that the communication between the parties is considered delivered and reached other party if transmitted by fax or electronic means with proof of sending machine. The date of receiving the messages, notices or letters is the date of sending authenticated by the sending machine. All notices under this Agreement shall be deemed to have been duly given upon the mailing of the notice, postpaid to the addresses listed above, or upon the facsimile transmission, to the party entitled to such notice at the facsimile number set forth below.
perturbation.changed_text: X. Communication : The two parties agree that the communication between the parties is considered delivered and reached other party if transmitted by fax or electronic means with proof of sending machine, except for legal notices, which require registered mail. The date of receiving the messages, notices or letters is the date of sending authenticated by the sending machine. All notices under this Agreement shall be deemed to have been duly given upon the mailing of the notice, postpaid to the addresses listed above, or upon the facsimile transmission, to the party entitled to such notice at the facsimile number set forth below, except for legal notices.
perturbation.explanation: This modifies the communication clause by introducing an exception for legal notices, which now require registered mail, while earlier communications were acceptable through fax or electronic means. This introduces ambiguity and potential disputes about the validity of communications, particularly when legal notices are concerned. The original text in Section X accepts electronic comuication, and the new insertion adds the exception for legal purposes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: X. Communication : The two parties agree that the communication between the parties is considered delivered and reached other party if transmitted by fax or electronic means with proof of sending machine, except for legal notices, which require registered mail. The date of receiving the messages, notices or letters is the date of sending authenticated by the sending machine. All notices under this Agreement shall be deemed to have been duly given upon the mailing of the notice, postpaid to the addresses listed above, or upon the facsimile transmission, to the party entitled to such notice at the facsimile number set forth below, except for legal notices.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section X introduces an exception for legal notices, requiring them to be sent via registered mail, while the original clause allowed for communication via fax or electronic means. This creates a direct contradiction because the original text broadly states that communication can be delivered by electronic means but the changed text creates an exception for legal communication in the same section X. This conflicts with the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction

perturbation.original_text: XVIII. Governing Law . This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India and the Party’s country laws on equal force. If there is any conflict of laws, the law of India shall prevail.
perturbation.changed_text: XVIII. Governing Law . This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India and the Party’s country laws on equal force. If there is any conflict of laws, the law most favorable to the Receiving Party shall prevail.
perturbation.explanation: This change modifies the governing law clause to state that in the event of a conflict of laws, the law most favorable to the Receiving Party shall prevail. This directly contradicts the original clause, which stated that the law of India would prevail in such conflicts. The new insertion contradicts what would happen in conflict of laws.
perturbation.contradicted_text: XVIII. Governing Law . This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India and the Party’s country laws on equal force. If there is any conflict of laws, the law most favorable to the Receiving Party shall prevail.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the governing law clause, stating that in case of a conflict of laws, the law most favorable to the Receiving Party shall prevail. This directly contradicts the original clause which stated that the law of India would prevail in such conflicts, leading to a clear in-text contradiction as per the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'. The contradicted text is the exact same text as the changed text, because the contradiction is self-contained within the clause itself by contradicting the original intent.
